mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar S-Type 2.5 V6 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned S-Type 2.5 V6 (AJ25) engine typically costs £3,200–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Engine Finders reports savings of up to £1,800 versus main dealer pricing. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar S-Type 2.5 V6 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ25 unit is quite reliable, but it has well-documented weaknesses. The main problems are associated with timing chain tensioner failures (shared across the AJ-V6 and AJ-V8 families), air leaks due to drying gaskets, VCT solenoid clogging, cooling system issues (more prevalent on the 2.5 than the 3.0), and oil consumption at high mileage. Regular maintenance is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar S-Type 2.5 V6?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure (the defining weak point of the AJ-V6 family), VCT solenoid clogging, cooling system leaks (hoses, header tank, thermostat), air leaks from drying gaskets, crankcase ventilation valve issues, and oil consumption at high mileage. See the Era Map above for detailed advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar S-Type 2.5 V6 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept S-Type (values £3,000–£10,000+ depending on condition and mileage), a £3,500–£4,500 reconditioned engine is 35–70% of the vehicle's value — often worth it for a solid example. The Jaguar scrap threshold of 60% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar S-Type 2.5 V6 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code AJ25 is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 2.5 V6 was the entry-level petrol engine, offered from 2001 until the S-Type was replaced by the XF in 2007. It is a 2.5-litre V6 from the Duratec V6 family, producing 200 bhp. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the AJ25 timing chain, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain, guide and tensioner replacement costs £1,200–£3,000. Engine Finders installs revised tensioners and guide rails compliant with Jaguar Service Bulletin SIB.037.18. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£3,200–£6,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar S‑Type 2.5 V6 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: AJ25 engines are no longer produced new; reconditioned units are the standard

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the S‑Type 2.5 V6 AJ25 era (2001–2007). Engine Finders reports savings of up to £1,800 versus main dealer pricing on S‑Type engine replacements. Customers have reported dealer quotes of around £15,000–£30,000 for engine replacement, while reconditioning is often in the £8,000–£10,000 range.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an S‑Type 2.5 V6 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The V6 layout and longitudinal mounting make the engine reasonably accessible, but timing chain work requires significant disassembly. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare S‑Type 2.5 V6 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure (AJ25)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic clattering from the front of the engine, engine warning light, loss of power, potential engine failure if chain skips or snaps.
problems.replacementCost: £3,200–£6,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: All Jaguar S‑Type variants (X200/X250 chassis) share timing chain tensioner failures (OEM LR108765) due to shared AJ‑V6/AJ‑V8 architecture. Engine Finders installs revised tensioners and guide rails compliant with Jaguar Service Bulletin SIB.037.18, eliminating premature wear and catastrophic chain failure. A cold‑start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston‑valve contact and a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes are the best prevention. The chain should last the life of the engine with proper maintenance.

problems.title: Variable Camshaft Timing (VCT) Solenoid Failure (AJ25)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, loss of power at low RPM, poor fuel economy, check engine light.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can affect valve timing and engine performance

problems.recommendation: AJ‑V6 and AJ‑V8 engines across all S‑Type variants suffer from VCT solenoid clogging (OEM LR223456) causing rough idle and power loss. We replace these with upgraded OEM‑spec solenoids and perform full phaser inspections during timing chain service. If you experience rough running, have the VCT system inspected promptly.

problems.title: Cooling System & Coolant Leaks (AJ25)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, intermittent low coolant warning, temperature gauge fluctuations, sweet smell from engine bay, overheating.
problems.repairCost: £300–£900 (coolant hose, header tank, thermostat or water pump replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: £3,200–£6,500 (if overheating leads to head gasket damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss or overheating occurs

problems.recommendation: Cooling system problems are more of an issue on the 2.5 than the 3.0. Common causes include small leaks in hoses, faulty header tanks, heater control valve leaks, failing thermostats, water pump issues, and in the worst case, head gasket failure. If you notice coolant loss or temperature fluctuations, have the cooling system pressure‑tested immediately. Replacing the header tank and checking for small hose leaks is a good starting point for diagnosis.

problems.title: Air Leaks & Vacuum System Issues (AJ25)
problems.symptoms: Erratic idle, rough running, stalling, poor fuel economy, engine warning light.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can affect drivability

problems.recommendation: The main problems with the AJ25 are associated with air leaks due to drying gaskets. The electrically driven geometry change system often fails in the intake. The crankcase ventilation valve (CCV) requires regular cleaning or the lubricant will press from all the cracks. If you experience erratic idle or rough running, have the intake and vacuum system inspected for leaks and the CCV valve cleaned or replaced.

problems.title: Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Issues (AJ25)
problems.symptoms: Low oil warning light, needing to top up oil frequently, blue smoke from exhaust, ticking noise from engine.
problems.repairCost: £150–£300 (regular oil top‑ups and monitoring) — £1,000–£3,000+ (piston ring replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: £3,200–£6,500 (if oil starvation leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; monitor oil levels weekly

problems.recommendation: At high mileage, oil consumption occurs due to the fault of stuck piston rings. The unit has many rare and expensive parts, so a full rebuild can be costly. Check your oil level regularly — this engine is not one you can neglect. Use the correct grade of fully synthetic oil (5W‑30 is recommended) and change it regularly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar S‑Type 2.5 V6 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Straightforward replacement; catch it before overheating
Viable if caught early; often requires full rebuild
Piston‑valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This S‑Type 2.5 V6? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using 5W‑30 oil
* Timing chain tensioners replaced or inspected (upgraded units preferred)
* No cold‑start rattle — timing chain should be healthy
* Cooling system in good order (no coolant loss, recent header tank or hoses)
* VCT system functioning correctly (smooth idle, no hesitation)
avoidIf: * No proof of regular maintenance — the AJ25 is durable but unforgiving of neglect
* Cold‑start rattle present — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* Coolant loss with no diagnosis — cooling system issues are common on the 2.5
* Oil consumption of 1L per 1,000 miles or more — piston ring wear likely
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — the AJ25 can hide expensive faults

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Jaguar S‑Type 2.5 V6 is an elegant classic saloon with a smooth, refined V6 engine from the Ford Duratec family. The AJ25 is a 200‑bhp, 2.5‑litre V6 that offers adequate performance and a characterful soundtrack. However, it has well‑documented weaknesses: timing chain tensioner failure (shared across the AJ‑V6 and AJ‑V8 families), VCT solenoid clogging, cooling system issues (more prevalent on the 2.5 than the 3.0), air leaks from drying gaskets, and oil consumption at high mileage. The S‑Type itself has a reputation for electrical gremlins and some corrosion on chassis components. Buy on service history, prioritise a car with upgraded timing chain tensioners, and budget for preventative cooling system maintenance. This is a stylish classic that rewards careful ownership but punishes neglect.

cta.label: → Compare S‑Type 2.5 V6 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar S‑Type 2.5 V6 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle from the front of the engine (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Service History — Regular oil changes using 5W‑30 oil are essential for the AJ25
* Timing Chain Work — Have the tensioners been upgraded? (critical for higher‑mileage cars)
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates potential cooling system issues
* Idle Quality — Any roughness? VCT solenoid or air leak issues may be present
* Oil Level — Check dipstick; excessive consumption indicates piston ring wear
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, VCT, or cooling system codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (often related to fuel system or VCT issues)
* Electrics & Body — S‑Types are known for electrical gremlins and some chassis corrosion
